BigBoss Commerce  ·  Operator Guide  ·  Inventory · Procure-to-Pay

Purchase Orders

The full procure-to-pay flow in one screen: raise a purchase order, receive the goods into stock, then post the vendor bill with a built-in 3-way match that catches over-billing before A/P is booked. Everything a buyer needs — order, receipt, bill, payment — without leaving the list.

10 steps Branch Head Office (HO-001) callout = where to click EN  ·  translation-ready

Flow — inventory-purchase-orders

  1. 01 Open Purchase Orders
  2. →
  3. 02 Filter by status and payment
  4. →
  5. 03 Raise a new purchase order
  6. →
  7. 04 Pick the supplier
  8. →
  9. 05 Set the payment terms
  10. →
  11. 06 Create the order
  12. →
  13. 07 Open a received PO
  14. →
  15. 08 Post the vendor bill
  16. →
  17. 09 3-way match: enter the vendor's actual invoice
  18. →
  19. 10 Book the payable
1

STEP 01·NAVIGATE

Open Purchase Orders

From the Inventory sidebar, select Purchase Orders. This is the buyer's home base — every PO for the active branch, from draft to received to fully paid, in one filterable list.

2

STEP 02·SEARCH

Filter by status and payment

Narrow the list fast: the Status filter (Draft / Approved / Received / Cancelled) and the Payment filter (Unpaid / Partial / Paid) stack, so 'Received + Unpaid' instantly surfaces exactly the orders that still owe a vendor bill.

3

STEP 03·CLICK

Raise a new purchase order

Click New Purchase Order. A side sheet opens to capture the supplier, terms, and the lines you're buying — no page reload, no separate screen.

4

STEP 04·CLICK

Pick the supplier

Choose the supplier the goods are coming from. The supplier drives the payable partner, the default credit terms, and — for designated buyers — whether VDS is withheld when the bill is paid.

5

STEP 05·SELECT

Set the payment terms

Cash purchases settle on receipt (no separate bill). Credit purchases accrue accounts payable — goods receipt books inventory against a GR/IR clearing account, and the vendor bill you post later clears it. Pick Credit for the standard bill-then-pay flow.

6

STEP 06·CLICK

Create the order

Add each line by barcode or SKU, then Create Purchase Order. It lands in Draft; approve it, and it's ready to receive when the goods arrive.

7

STEP 07·CLICK

Open a received PO

When goods arrive, receive the PO — stock posts to inventory and the GR/IR accrual is booked automatically. Then reopen it from the row's menu → View Details to bill it.

8

STEP 08·CLICK

Post the vendor bill

The detail sheet's action bar is pinned to the bottom, so Receive, Post Vendor Bill and Record Payment are always one click away — no scrolling past the line items. Click Post Vendor Bill to raise the payable.

9

STEP 09·TYPE

3-way match: enter the vendor's actual invoice

This is the control that stops overpaying suppliers. The field pre-fills with the PO total; type the amount on the vendor's real paper invoice instead. The system reconciles billed vs. PO vs. received — within tolerance it posts silently; beyond tolerance it warns or blocks, and the variance is stamped on the journal entry for audit.

10

STEP 10·CLICK

Book the payable

Post Bill clears the GR/IR accrual and credits Accounts Payable (net of any withheld VDS), booking claimable input VAT in the same entry. The bill is now a matched, aging-tracked payable ready for Record Payment.

Video / translation readyEvery step carries a single narration line, tagged data-i18n and mirrored in the embedded JSON below. Swap the narration.en strings for Bangla (or any locale) and drive a voiceover + screen-pan render straight from the step boxes.
